top_candidates.reasoning: The engineering layer literally quotes 'ajaḥ nityaḥ śāśvato 'yaṁ purāṇaḥ' in the concrete scenario, names purāṇa as the verse's distinctive contribution (ancient), and structures the analog around temporal precedence (need predates codebase / company / language). This is BG 2.20's defining structure, near-verbatim from Kaṭha Upaniṣad 1.2.18.
BG 11.18 has 'sanātanas tvaṁ puruṣo mato me' — naming the Lord as eternal Person. Distantly related to purāṇa but the analog focuses on substrate antiquity, not on the Lord's identity.
BG 8.9 names the Supreme as 'kavim purāṇam anuśāsitāram' (the ancient seer-ruler). Same purāṇa term but different rhetorical role. Distant secondary.
